articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Context
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5, featuring deeply integrated multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video inputs with a 1M-token effective context window. The model demonstrates significant improvements on graduate-level science benchmarks and complex multi-step coding tasks, narrowing the gap with specialized domain models. Enterprise API availability began rolling out this week with tiered pricing based on context usage.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics Models Enable Zero-Shot Manipulation in Unstructured Environments
articles.summary: DeepMind published results from its Gemini Robotics Foundation Model program, showing humanoid and multi-arm robots completing novel warehouse pick-and-pack tasks with no task-specific fine-tuning. The system combines Gemini's world model with a learned physics prior, achieving 94% grasp success on previously unseen objects. The work signals a meaningful step toward general-purpose robotic manipulation outside controlled lab settings.

articles.title: Meta Releases Llama 4 Herd of Models Under Permissive Open License
articles.summary: Meta released the Llama 4 family including a 400B-parameter dense model and a mixture-of-experts variant exceeding 1T parameters, both under an updated open-use license that permits commercial deployment. Benchmark results show the MoE model competing with GPT-5 on reasoning and code generation while requiring significantly less inference compute per token. The release intensifies the open-weight vs. closed-model debate as frontier capability becomes more accessible.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics Atlas Begins Commercial Pilot Deployments in Automotive Manufacturing
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics announced that its electric Atlas humanoid robot has entered paid pilot programs at three major automotive OEMs, performing sub-assembly, part transfer, and quality inspection tasks alongside human workers. The company reported Atlas operating autonomously for 8-hour shifts with a mean-time-between-intervention exceeding 45 minutes. This marks the first large-scale commercial revenue milestone for a general-purpose humanoid platform.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production on A14 2nm Process Node Ahead of Schedule
articles.summary: TSMC confirmed volume production has started on its N2 (2nm) gate-all-around transistor process, roughly one quarter ahead of the previously communicated timeline. Early yield data reportedly exceeds initial projections, with Apple and NVIDIA as anchor customers for the first production wave. The node promises roughly 15% speed improvement and 30% power reduction over N3E, critical for next-generation AI accelerator and mobile SoC designs.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Constitutional Classifiers and Real-Time Auditing for Enterprise Claude Deployments
articles.summary: Anthropic launched a new safety infrastructure layer for Claude enterprise customers that includes constitutional classifiers capable of flagging policy-violating outputs in under 50ms and a real-time auditing dashboard for compliance teams. The system allows organizations to define domain-specific constitutions that shape model behavior without fine-tuning, addressing a major enterprise adoption blocker. Early adopters in financial services and healthcare reported a 70% reduction in manual output review overhead.

articles.title: Neuralink Receives FDA Clearance for Second-Generation Brain-Computer Interface Implant
articles.summary: The FDA granted Neuralink clearance to begin broader clinical trials with its N2 implant, which doubles the electrode count to 2,048 channels and features a fully wireless power system eliminating the external charging puck. Three participants from the initial cohort demonstrated cursor control and robotic arm manipulation with latencies under 100ms. The approval expands the trial to 50 patients with quadriplegia across ten clinical sites in the United States.

articles.title: Figure AI and BMW Expand Humanoid Robot Deployment to Full Assembly Line Integration
articles.summary: Figure AI announced the expansion of its Figure 02 humanoid robot program at BMW's Spartanburg plant from a single-station pilot to integrated multi-station deployment across a body-shop assembly line. The robots now coordinate with each other and existing automation via a shared task planner powered by a fine-tuned vision-language model. BMW reported a measurable throughput increase and stated plans to deploy over 100 units across its global manufacturing network by year-end.
